summary.intent: Address a batch of Codex review comments on PR #665 in fogodev/ars-ui, fixing selection logic bugs in the listbox and select components, then get CI green and Codex approval.
summary.outcome: Commit 31f34d31 was pushed fixing nine issues (selectable-key guards, structural-key no-ops, monotonic timestamp fallbacks, range-extension anchor fallback, case-insensitive Ctrl+A). A second patch was then committed for six more Codex findings (replace-mode activation, Set::All len pre-check removal, real host monotonic timestamps). All selection tests, proptest, i18n check, and clippy passed locally before the final push.

repo: Listbox and Select share a 'selectable-key guard' pattern: structural/non-focusable keys must be rejected before any selection mutation (toggle, deselect, SelectItem, DeselectItem).
Set::All must be preserved when DeselectItem receives a structural key — the guard must no-op before materializing Set::All.
Default keydown helpers should emit typeahead with a real host monotonic timestamp fallback; the _at field on events is reserved for adapter-provided/deterministic timestamps (e.g., tests).
Range extension (Shift+Arrow) must fall back to the current highlighted item when no valid anchor exists.
Ctrl/Cmd+A keyboard matching must be case-insensitive to handle uppercase 'A' events.
Codex is used as an automated reviewer via '@codex review' comments on the PR; threads are resolved via GitHub GraphQL resolveReviewThread mutation.
CI pipeline order: snapshot → formatting → unit → adapter → DOM/i18n browser → integration → feature-core → coverage → release verification → feature-flag matrix.

code.finding: Toggle/deselect operations require a selectable focusable item key; structural group keys must be rejected here.
SelectItem rejects structural/non-focusable keys through the selectable-key guard.
DeselectItem no-ops for structural keys before deselect_from_all to preserve Set::All.
Range extension falls back to the current highlighted item when no valid anchor exists; patch adjusted this region.
Ctrl/Cmd+A matching made case-insensitive in this region.
Default listbox keydown helper updated to use real host monotonic timestamp fallback for typeahead events.
Select trigger/content keydown helpers updated with monotonic timestamp fallback; DeselectItem rejects structural keys before materializing Set::All.
Select selection uses the same selectable-key guard as listbox.

workflow: Resolving Codex review threads requires both a reply comment (gh api POST pulls/comments with in_reply_to) and a separate GraphQL resolveReviewThread mutation — reply alone does not close the thread.
After pushing fixes, '@codex review' must be re-triggered manually via a PR comment.
sleep + gh pr checks polling was used to wait for CI stages to complete between fix batches.
PROPTEST_CASES=256 and --ignored flag are needed to run proptest state machine tests for this crate.
cargo +nightly fmt --all is required for formatting; clippy runs with -D warnings to enforce no warnings policy.

friction: Multiple Codex review batches required iterative fix-push-wait cycles rather than a single pass, due to Codex not surfacing all issues at once.
CI pipeline has many sequential stages (coverage, release verification, feature-flag matrix) that extend the feedback loop even after core tests pass.

The synthetic timestamp fallback in keydown helpers required two separate fix attempts — first batch used a monotonic fallback Codex still objected to, requiring a second patch to use real host monotonic time.
Resolving review threads requires two separate API calls (reply + GraphQL mutation), adding overhead per thread.
open_items: Codex had not yet posted a thumbs-up approval after the second patch was pushed — further review rounds may still be pending.
Coverage, release verification, and feature-flag matrix CI jobs were still pending at the time of the last recorded CI snapshot.
The replace-mode activation fix and Set::All len pre-check removal from the second batch should be verified in the next Codex review cycle.
